name: Raspberry-Peach Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 cups peaches, fresh or frozen, thawed, drained, reserving juices
2 cups raspberries, fresh or frozen, thawed, drained, reserving juices
1/2 cup hazelnuts, pecans, or walnuts, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
2. Roll out dough for the bottom crust and place it in a 9-inch pie pan. Trim. Chill.
3. To make the filling, mix the flour and sugar together in a bowl.
4. Add the drained peaches, raspberries, nuts, and optional rum to the flour and sugar mixture. Stir until well mixed.
5. Stir in half of the reserved fruit juices until the filling is evenly moistened.
6. Place the filling into the chilled crust.
7. Dot the top of the filling with butter pieces.
8. To add a top crust, brush the edges of the bottom crust with water to help it adhere.
9. Press on lattice dough or place a full crust over the filling, making sure it's secure and sealed.
10. Brush the crust with the glaze (sugar dissolved in water).
11. Bake for 3/4 hour in the hot oven, or until the crust is golden brown and juices are bubbling.
